messages.content: Folks keep experiencing involuntary disconnections — mid-workflow they get bounced to the authentication prompt. No notification, no dialog. What mechanism forcibly revokes their ongoing credential?

function.name: search_knowledge_base
function.arguments: {"query":"users kicked out app randomly logged out"}

messages.name: search_knowledge_base
messages.content:
[chunk 01 · ops/deploy-runbook.md · similarity 0.42]
Production deployments follow a blue-green strategy. The new version is brought up behind the load balancer, health checks run for 60 seconds, then traffic is drained from the old pods over 30 seconds. Rollback is triggered automatically if the error rate exceeds 2% in the first five minutes. Deploy windows are Tuesday and Thursday 10:00–14:00 UTC; off-window deploys require VP approval and an on-call shadow.

[chunk 02 · billing/subscription-lifecycle.md · similarity 0.40]
When a subscription lapses (payment retry exhausted after 4 attempts over 14 days), the account enters a 7-day grace period. During grace the user can still log in and view data but cannot create new resources. After grace, the account is suspended: API calls return 403 and the dashboard shows a reactivation banner. Data is retained for 90 days post-suspension.

[chunk 03 · security/session-management.md · similarity 0.38]
The platform enforces a strict concurrent-session policy. When max_concurrent_sessions=1 (the default for standard-tier accounts), a new login from a different device or browser triggers session eviction triggered by concurrent_login_policy on the older session. The evicted session's refresh token is revoked server-side and the next API call returns 401, causing the client to redirect to /login. There is no user-facing notification for the evicted session — the user simply finds themselves at the login screen. Enterprise accounts can raise the limit to 5 via the admin console.

[chunk 04 · eng/api-rate-limits.md · similarity 0.37]
Public API endpoints are rate-limited at 100 requests per minute per API key using a sliding-window counter in Redis. Exceeding the limit returns 429 Too Many Requests with a Retry-After header. Internal service-to-service calls use a separate pool with a 1000 rpm limit. Rate-limit metrics are exported to Prometheus under api_rate_limit_exceeded_total.

[chunk 05 · it/vpn-troubleshooting.md · similarity 0.36]
If the VPN tunnel drops repeatedly, check the WireGuard handshake timestamp: a stale handshake (>180s) usually means the endpoint IP changed (laptop moved networks). Restart the tunnel or toggle airplane mode. Split-tunnel routes only 10.0.0.0/8 through the VPN; public sites should not be affected. Persistent failures may indicate a firewall blocking UDP 51820.

[chunk 06 · security/identity-provider.md · similarity 0.35]
SSO is backed by an external IdP (Okta or Azure AD). When the IdP rotates its SAML signing certificate, the platform must import the new certificate within the overlap window (typically 24–72 hours). If the old certificate expires before import, all SSO logins fail with a signature-verification error and existing sessions whose refresh tokens were issued under the old certificate have their auth context invalidated during IdP SAML certificate rotation — the token-refresh call returns 401 and the user is redirected to re-authenticate. Self-service certificate upload is available at /admin/sso/certificates.

[chunk 07 · eng/frontend-error-handling.md · similarity 0.34]
The SPA catches unhandled promise rejections globally and reports them to Sentry with the current route and user ID. Network errors (fetch failures, timeouts) show a toast notification. A 401 response from any API call triggers the auth-redirect interceptor, which clears local tokens and navigates to /login. The interceptor debounces to prevent redirect loops when multiple parallel requests all return 401 simultaneously.

[chunk 08 · ops/incident-postmortem-template.md · similarity 0.33]
Postmortems are required for SEV1 and SEV2 incidents within 5 business days. Use this template: Title, Date, Duration, Impact (users affected, revenue impact), Timeline (detect → mitigate → resolve), Root Cause (5-whys), Contributing Factors, Action Items (each with owner and due date). Blameless by policy — focus on systems, not individuals.

[chunk 09 · hr/remote-work-policy.md · similarity 0.33]
Full-time employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week. Core collaboration hours are 10:00–15:00 in the employee's local timezone. Equipment stipend of $1,500 is provided on hire for home-office setup. Internet reimbursement of $75/month requires a minimum 50 Mbps connection. Fully remote positions require director approval.

[chunk 10 · eng/database-migrations.md · similarity 0.32]
Schema migrations run as part of the deploy pipeline via Flyway. Each migration is a versioned SQL file in db/migrations/. Backward-incompatible changes (column drops, type changes) must be split into expand-and-contract phases: first deploy adds the new column/table, backfill runs, then a second deploy drops the old. Migrations must complete within 30 seconds to avoid lock contention.

[chunk 11 · billing/payment-methods.md · similarity 0.32]
Supported payment methods: credit/debit card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex), ACH direct debit (US only), wire transfer (invoiced accounts, $10k+ annual). Card tokenization is handled by Stripe; we never store raw card numbers. Failed charges retry on days 1, 3, 7, 14 with exponential backoff. After 4 failures the subscription enters grace (see subscription-lifecycle.md).

[chunk 12 · ops/monitoring-alerting.md · similarity 0.31]
Alerts are routed through PagerDuty. Critical alerts (p99 latency > 2s, error rate > 5%, pod crash loop) page the on-call immediately. Warning alerts (disk > 80%, memory > 90%, certificate expiry < 30 days) go to Slack #ops-alerts. All metrics are retained in Prometheus for 30 days and downsampled to Thanos for 1 year.

[chunk 13 · eng/feature-flags.md · similarity 0.31]
Feature flags are managed via LaunchDarkly. New features default to off in production. Roll-out follows: 1% canary for 24h, 10% for 48h, 50% for 24h, then 100%. Kill-switch flags can be toggled by any engineer; percentage-based flags require a team lead. Stale flags (unchanged for 90 days) are flagged for cleanup in the weekly triage.

[chunk 14 · it/laptop-provisioning.md · similarity 0.30]
New hires receive a MacBook Pro (M-series) or ThinkPad X1 Carbon (Linux). Standard builds include: OS hardening profile, full-disk encryption, endpoint protection agent, WireGuard VPN profile, and the company CA certificate. Laptops are refreshed every 36 months. Return old devices to IT within 2 weeks of receiving the replacement.

[chunk 15 · eng/code-review-guidelines.md · similarity 0.30]
Every PR needs one approval from a code owner. Reviews should complete within one business day. Focus on: correctness, security implications, test coverage, and readability. Nit-picks should be prefixed with "nit:" so the author can batch them. Draft PRs are exempt from the review SLA but should be marked as such.

[chunk 16 · security/access-control.md · similarity 0.29]
RBAC is enforced at the API gateway level. Roles: viewer (read-only), editor (read + write own resources), admin (full access + user management), super-admin (billing + SSO config). Role assignments are audited and reviewed quarterly. Service accounts use scoped API keys with explicit permission grants.

[chunk 17 · ops/capacity-planning.md · similarity 0.29]
Quarterly capacity reviews compare trailing-90-day resource utilization against provisioned capacity. Target headroom is 40% for compute and 30% for storage. Autoscaling handles short bursts (HPA scales pods on CPU > 70% avg over 5 minutes). Long-term growth (>20% QoQ) triggers a procurement request for additional node pools.

[chunk 18 · eng/testing-strategy.md · similarity 0.28]
Test pyramid: unit tests (>80% coverage target), integration tests (key workflows), E2E tests (critical user journeys via Playwright). Tests run in CI on every PR; merges blocked if any tier fails. Flaky tests are quarantined after 3 consecutive false positives and assigned to the responsible team. Load tests run weekly against staging.

[chunk 19 · hr/onboarding-checklist.md · similarity 0.28]
Day 1: laptop setup, email/Slack/GitHub access, security training enrollment, meet the buddy. Week 1: team intro sessions, codebase walkthrough, first PR (starter task). Month 1: complete security training, ship first feature, 30-day check-in with manager. Quarter 1: 90-day review, identify growth areas, set OKRs.

[chunk 20 · facilities/office-access.md · similarity 0.27]
Badge access is active 06:00–22:00 weekdays. Weekend and after-hours access requires a manager-approved request submitted at least 24 hours in advance. Visitors must be pre-registered and escorted at all times. Lost badges must be reported to facilities within 24 hours; a temporary badge is issued within 2 hours of the report.
